MEMORANDUM
Date: December 13, 1990
To:
RE:
From: T. Padberg a.P
Protest of Closed Session Scheduled to be Conducted as Part of Special
Meeting of Golf Course Committee of the City of San Clemente on December
13, 1990 at 5:00 PM.
tttt+t 
t * a * t * * * + t t * a * at a* * * a a t *t**:l* 
* a*l* i t t I t + a +t a + t * * t 3* ** *:l t I *
Please consider this memorandum to be my official protest of the subiect closed session
because the subiect closed session violates the Brown Act ot the State of California.
The subiect closed session was announced as follows on 12-t2-90: 'Closed session to
discuss property negotiations pursuant to Government Code Section 54956.8' Meeting
with Mayor Diehl and the Golf Course Committee regarding property negotiations as they
relate to the proposed Foothill Corridor. Property located in Camp Pendleton - State
Parks Leasehold. Negotiator - (USMC)."
The Code Section pursuant to which lhis closed session is being called reads as follows:
"Notwithstanding any olher provision ot this Chapter, a legislative body ot a local agency
may hold a closed session with its negotiator prior to the purchase, sale, exchange or
lease of real properly, by or for the iocai agency, to give instructions io its negotiaror
regarding the price, terms of payment for the purchase, sale, exchange or lease-
However, prior to the closed session, the legislative body of the local agency shall hold
an open and public session at which it identifies the real property or real properties to
be negotiated which the negotiations may concern, and the person or persons with whom
its negotiator may negotiate- For purposes of this section, the negotiator may be a
member of the legislative body at the local agency; for purposes of this section, lease
includes renewal or renegotiation ot the lease. Nothing in this section shall preclude the
local agency from holding a closed session for discussions regarding eminent domain
proceedings pursuant to Section 54956.9." (emphasis added.)
The proposed closed session is illegal for the following reasons:
1) the required prior public session has not been held;
2) the referenced Code Section empowers legislative bodies ONLY to hold closed
session for the purpose(s) stated therein. The Golf Course Committee is NOT a
legislative body; refer to Section 1, Besolution 84-81 of the City Council of the
Caty of San Clemente, which defines the powers and duties of the Golf Course
Committee (copy attached).
all City Council members;
Col. E. J. Turse, USMC - Camp Pendleton;
A. Oliver, Dist. Supt., CA Dept. of Recreation and parksi
Fair Political Practices Commission
Attorney General, State of California


NOTICE AND CALL OF SPECIAL MEETING
OF THE GOLF COMMITTEE
TO MEMBERS OF THE SAN CLEMENTE GOLF COURSE COMMITTEE
NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that a special meeting of the San Clemente
Golf Course Committee is hereby called to be held on Thursday, December 13, 1990, at 5:00
PM, in the Mayor's Cont'erence Room, City Hall, 100 Ave Presidio, San Clemente, CA.
92672.
Said special meeting shall be held to consider the following agenda item(s).
(lf not stated here or on agenda, it cannot be discussed.)
1. Closed session to discuss property negotiations pursuant to Goverment Code Section
54956.8. Meeting with Mayor Diehl and the Colf Course Committee regarding property
negotiations as they relate to the proposed Foothill corridor. Property located in camp
Pendelton - State Parks leasehold. Negotiator- (USMC)
DATED:

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a closed session of the Golf Course Committee regarding property negotiations violates the Brown Act because no prior public session was held and the Golf Course Committee is not a legislative body authorized to conduct such closed sessions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/protest memorandum to the FPPC, not an FPPC response letter. No FPPC conclusion or advice section is present in the document. The document includes the protest memo, notice of special meeting, and relevant resolution establishing the Golf Course Committee's powers. Addressed to multiple recipients including the FPPC, City Council, and other agencies.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether a closed session of the Golf Course Committee regarding property negotiations violates the Brown Act because no prior public session was held and the Golf Course Committee is not a legislative body authorized to conduct such closed sessions.

embedding.summary: This is a memorandum protesting a closed session scheduled by the Golf Course Committee of San Clemente on December 13, 1990, arguing that the closed session violates the Brown Act because the required prior public session was not held and the Golf Course Committee lacks statutory authority to conduct closed sessions for property negotiations.
